steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The colon introduces evidence from a corpus—"great" is the sixth most commonly used adjective—which directly confirms the linguist's assumption that "great" appears quite often in written English.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The linguist could ______ the assumption using corpus data — "great" being the sixth most common adjective is evidence that supports the assumption. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — entail: logically require or imply — the corpus data doesn't logically require the assumption, it provides evidence for it. ✗
  • Choice B — transpose: rearrange or swap — the linguist isn't rearranging anything. ✗
  • Choice C — refute: disprove or contradict — the corpus confirms the assumption, not contradicts it. ✗
  • Choice D — substantiate: back up with evidence — the corpus ranking of "great" directly supports the assumption that it appears often. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
